entries.title: Trump says US to pause operation to guide vessels through Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: "Project Freedom", which began less than 48 hours ago, will be halted because progress has been made toward a deal with Iran, the US president says.

entries.title: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
entries.snippet: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

entries.title: Vivek Ramaswamy wins Republican nomination for Ohio governor
entries.snippet: He spent much of the race criticising the state's response to the Covid-19 pandemic led by the Democrat nominee.

entries.title: Russian attacks kill more than 20 ahead of rival ceasefires proposed by Kyiv and Moscow
entries.snippet: Kyiv said it would begin a truce on 6 May and then "act symmetrically" after Moscow declared a pause for its Victory Day parade.

entries.title: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
entries.snippet: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

entries.title: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
entries.snippet: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

entries.title: Romanian PM ousted in no-confidence vote
entries.snippet: Ilie Bolojan lost the vote after the largest party in his coalition joined the far-right opposition to depose him.

entries.title: Apple to pay $250m to iPhone buyers over AI features lawsuit
entries.snippet: Claims from last year said the tech firm’s advertising of Apple Intelligence fooled iPhone buyers.

entries.title: A 'fun' superstar stuns rivals and reshapes politics in an Indian state
entries.snippet: Film star Vijay has defied naysayers to almost sweep Tamil Nadu polls, marking a break in the political order.

entries.title: Popular Australian author pleads guilty over child exploitation material
entries.snippet: The Jasper Jones and Runt author was arrested in January during a police raid at his Perth home.

entries.title: Rescuers among three dead after yacht sinks off Australian coast
entries.snippet: Volunteer rescuers aged 78 and 62 died after their boat rolled during "treacherous" conditions.

entries.title: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
entries.snippet: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

entries.title: Deadly China plane crash was caused by fuel cut-off, says report
entries.snippet: The Boeing 737 plane crashed into a Chinese hillside in 2022, killing all 132 people on board.

entries.title: Rapper Kid Cudi removes British star M.I.A. from tour
entries.snippet: British singer songwriter M.I.A will no longer be the opening act for Rebels Rage tour after disagreement with US rapper Kid Cudi.

entries.title: Ukrainian drone hits upmarket Moscow high-rise ahead of Victory Day celebrations
entries.snippet: Russia has declared a unilateral ceasefire to cover the event and threatened a massive strike on central Kyiv if it were disrupted.

entries.title: Three Russian diplomats expelled from Austria over spying accusations
entries.snippet: They gathered information using a "forest of antennas" installed on top of diplomatic buildings, the foreign minister says.

entries.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
entries.snippet: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

entries.title: Britney Spears pleads guilty to reckless driving after arrest
entries.snippet: The 44-year-old will avoid jail time after her arrest in California earlier this year.

entries.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani recovering from pneumonia and 'now breathing on his own'
entries.snippet: The ex-New York mayor 'remains in critical but stable condition', according to his spokesman.

entries.title: The most talked-about looks at this year's Met Gala
entries.snippet: The theme for 2026 Met Gala fundraiser is "Costume Art", exploring fashion as an embodied art form.

entries.title: Trump-backed Ramaswamy wins Ohio governor primary, setting up a competitive Nov. race
entries.snippet: In Ohio, where a Democrat hasn't won an election for governor in 20 years, Republican Vivek Ramaswamy and Democrat Amy Acton could be in for a tight race this fall.

entries.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
entries.snippet: State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.

entries.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
entries.snippet: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

entries.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
entries.snippet: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.

entries.title: From ICE detention to center stage: The Texas mariachi brothers opening for Kacey Musgraves
entries.snippet: The brothers and their parents were taken into federal custody earlier this year during a required immigration check-in. The story caught the ear of a country music icon.

entries.title: The oldest U.S. Jewish seminary, Hebrew Union College, shuts down a rabbinical program
entries.snippet: Hebrew Union College, the oldest Jewish seminary in the United States, was established in 1875 in Cincinnati by the founder of Reform Judaism in North America. The college has other campuses, but its rabbinical program in Cincinnati will shut down and graduate its last four stude

entries.title: Midwives saved his mom's life -- and inspired him to pursue the profession
entries.snippet: May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.

entries.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
entries.snippet: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

entries.title: A legal scholar and 'Backtalker' defends critical race theory -- a term she helped coin
entries.snippet: Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.

entries.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know
entries.snippet: Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.

entries.title: Iran remains defiant after U.S. launches new effort to reopen Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: Top U.S. officials said Tuesday that the ceasefire with Iran has not ended, despite exchanges of fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Both Rubio and Hegseth said the U.S. was in a new phase of operations designed to ensure safe passage for commercial vessels stuck in the Persian Gulf. B

entries.title: News Wrap: Senate GOP requests $1 billion in funding for Trump's ballroom
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, Senate Republicans are requesting $1 billion to fund security improvements for Trump's White House ballroom, Ukrainian and Russian forces are observing a temporary ceasefire for Victory Day celebrations, a fireworks plant explosion in central China kille

entries.title: New wave of Southern states scramble to redraw congressional maps ahead of midterms
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court ruling against drawing congressional maps to protect Black or other minority voters has sparked a new wave in the ongoing redistricting war. Congressional correspondent Lisa Desjardins joins Amna Nawaz to discuss where things stand and what it means.

entries.title: Can hantavirus spread between humans? What to know as WHO investigates ship outbreak
entries.snippet: Three people are dead and nearly 150 remained quarantined Tuesday on a cruise ship off the coast of West Africa, as the World Health Organization investigates an outbreak of the rare but deadly hantavirus on board. William Brangham speaks with Dr. Céline Gounder, an infectious di

entries.title: Families in Lebanon still search rubble for loved ones killed in a day of Israeli strikes
entries.snippet: Tomorrow marks four weeks since the day known as "Black Wednesday" in Lebanon. The Israeli military, claiming to be targeting Hezbollah militants, unleashed an unprecedented aerial campaign on April 8 that killed more than 350 people in a matter of minutes. Special correspondent

entries.title: Thousands of immigrant truckers lose commercial licenses in Trump administration crackdown
entries.snippet: In March, around 200,000 immigrants began losing their commercial driver's licenses, which are required to operate large vehicles like semi-trucks, buses and tractor-trailers. It's part of a series of moves by the Trump administration to limit who can drive those vehicles after s

entries.title: How author Douglas Stuart's journey to a remote Scottish island inspired 'John of John'
entries.snippet: Douglas Stuart won the 2020 Booker Prize for his debut novel "Shuggie Bain," about a boy in 1980s Glasgow caring for his mother struggling with alcoholism. His latest novel "John of John," out today, follows a young man returning to his hometown on a rural Scottish island and gra

entries.title: Why Wynton Marsalis thinks jazz is the perfect metaphor for democracy
entries.snippet: Renowned trumpeter and composer Wynton Marsalis has launched a new project, a kind of call and response for these times. Senior arts correspondent Jeffrey Brown met Marsalis at the Jazz at Lincoln Center, for our series Art in Action, exploring the intersection of art and democra

entries.title: The Rolling Stones are releasing a new album titled 'Foreign Tongues'
entries.snippet: The Rolling Stones announced a new album on Tuesday. It is titled "Foreign Tongues." It will arrive July 10 and feature the late Charlie Watts, Steve Winwood, Paul McCartney, The Cure's Robert Smith and The Red Hot Chili Peppers' Chad Smith.

entries.title: U.S. gasoline prices rise 50% since the start of the Iran war
entries.snippet: The main reason drivers are paying more at the pump is because of the global energy crisis caused by the Iran War. The price of crude oil, which is the main ingredient in gasoline, has been climbing for most of the past two months because the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: Brazil gold mining rush brings deforestation and mercury risks to the Amazon
entries.snippet: SAO PAULO (AP) — The surge in gold prices in recent years has fueled a renewed mining rush in Brazil's Amazon rainforest, accelerating deforestation in protected areas and driving mercury contamination to hazardous levels, officials and experts say. A study released Tuesday by th

entries.title: Trump revives Presidential Physical Fitness Award for U.S. schools
entries.snippet: The award is tied to the Presidential Fitness Test, which was a public-school fixture for decades but was phased out under President Barack Obama in favor of a program that minimized competition and focused on long-term health. Trump signed an order last summer to reestablish the

entries.title: Police say shooting at shopping center outside Dallas leaves 2 dead, 3 injured
entries.snippet: It was not a random act of violence and the attacker knew the victims, Carrollton Police Chief Roberto Arredondo said.

entries.title: New Hampshire man who works for FAA accused of threatening to kill Trump
entries.snippet: Dean DelleChiaie, 35, of Nashua, was expected to make an initial court appearance Tuesday on a charge of interstate communication of a threat against the president

entries.title: U.S. job openings largely unchanged in March despite Iran conflict
entries.snippet: Employers posted 6.87 million jobs in March, compared to 6.92 million in February, the Labor Department reported Tuesday.

entries.title: Documenting recent vehicle-ramming incidents worldwide
entries.snippet: Two people were killed and three others were seriously injured when a driver plowed into people in a busy shopping area in the German city of Leipzig Monday in what officials believe was a deliberate attack. An unspecified number of people sustained less serious injuries, and the

entries.title: White House says Trump's deals with pharmaceutical companies offer billions in savings
entries.snippet: WASHINGTON (AP) — White House economists estimate that President Donald Trump's deals with pharmaceutical companies to drop some of their U.S. prescription drug prices to what they charge in other countries could save $529 billion over the next 10 years. The analysis obtained by

entries.title: Medical teams visit cruise ship passengers dealing with hantavirus outbreak
entries.snippet: Three passengers have died and at least four people are sick in what health officials say is an outbreak of hantavirus, which usually spreads by inhaling contaminated rodent droppings.

entries.title: UN resolution proposal would hit Iran with sanctions for halting freedom of navigation
entries.snippet: The draft resolution, co-sponsored by the United States and Gulf nations and obtained Tuesday by The Associated Press, also demands that Iran "immediately participate in and enable" United Nations efforts to establish a humanitarian corridor in the strait to enable the delivery o

entries.title: Russian strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before looming ceasefire
entries.snippet: On Tuesday afternoon, powerful Russian glide bombs smashed into the eastern city of Kramatorsk, the southern city of Zaporizhzhia and the northern city of Chernihiv, killing at least 17 civilians and wounding 45 others, officials said.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Spain to receive hantavirus-hit ship in Canary Islands
entries.snippet: Three passengers abord the MV Hondius have died after a hantavirus outbreak onboard. The cruise ship has been stuck off the coast of Cape Verde since the virus was detected.

entries.title: Iran war: Will tensions escalate in the Strait of Hormuz?
entries.snippet: Could US President Trump's "Project Freedom" clear the congestion in the Strait of Hormuz? Or could it lead to an escalation of hostilities? Questions and answers.

entries.title: Strategic neutrality: How Syria is winning in the Iran war
entries.snippet: Syria's new government has deliberately not become directly involved in the Iran war. It's presenting itself as neutral and as a possible solution to the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

entries.title: India: West Bengal election win boosts Modi's BJP
entries.snippet: The election outcome allows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party to further expand its political dominance.

entries.title: Boko Haram militants kill at least 23 troops in Chad attack
entries.snippet: Chad's President Mahamat Idriss Deby Itno vowed to eradicate Boko Haram after the "cowardly" attack. Boko Haram is one of several armed groups operating around Lake Chad.

entries.title: Armenia balances on the tightrope between Russia and the EU
entries.snippet: Armenia is moving closer to the EU politically and strategically. But its clear orientation toward the West while keeping close ties with Russia remains a balancing act.

entries.title: Here's why Amsterdam banned fossil fuel and meat advertising
entries.snippet: Cities from Amsterdam to Sydney are banning fossil fuel advertisements, drawing from tactics once used to curb smoking. They're targeting the messaging that has normalized carbon-intensive lifestyles for decades.

entries.title: Germany: Who is CDU parliamentary leader Jens Spahn?
entries.snippet: CDU firebrand, ex-health minister, and leader of his party's parliamentary group, Jens Spahn has been dogged by controversy. But his ambition is undimmed.

entries.title: Iran war: US says ceasefire still holds despite clashes
entries.snippet: The US military has again warned it will protect commercial shipping against Iranian attack in the Strait of Hormuz. However, US officials underlined that the ceasefire is still in place.

entries.title: Germany prepares for mission in the Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The German minesweeper Fulda has set course for the Mediterranean. The German armed forces may assist in clearing mines in the Strait of Hormuz — although only under certain conditions.

entries.title: Venice Biennale in crisis: The controversies explained
entries.snippet: The traditional Golden Lion will not be awarded this year, following the resignation of the jury over Russia's and Israel's disputed participation.

entries.title: Germany: BioNTech shutting down COVID vaccine production entirely, 1,860 jobs at risk
entries.snippet: The German company that pioneered one of the first mRNA coronavirus vaccines is halting production, handing the reins to partner Pfizer. BioNTech is restructuring to focus on research and illnesses like cancer.

entries.title: Japan pivots Indo-Pacific strategy toward Southeast Asia
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi used a visit to Vietnam to update Japan's Indo-Pacific strategy, putting energy security, critical minerals and ASEAN resilience at its center.

entries.title: Germany: Difficult first year for Chancellor Friedrich Merz
entries.snippet: A year after taking office, Germany's federal leadership is struggling with the drudgery of coalition governance. Whenever there are problems, the coalition partners are blamed. Or US President Donald Trump.

entries.title: What is Hantavirus?
entries.snippet: Three people have died on a cruise ship in the Atlantic, reportedly from hantavirus. The disease has been confirmed in another traveler. What are the symptoms of the virus, and how does infection occur?

entries.title: Romania's government collapses after Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan loses confidence vote
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has been voted out of office by lawmakers. Romania's coalition was driven apart by unpopular austerity measures, with the Social Democrats leaving and moving to oust the PM.

entries.title: Germany: Couple admits €1.9 million parking meter thefts
entries.snippet: A German couple accused of siphoning large quantities of cash from parking meters has confessed in court. Nearly two million euros disappeared from machines in a picturesque Bavarian town — but no one noticed for years.

entries.title: Decoding China: Beijing courts Europe amid US dysfunction
entries.snippet: China is hoping to secure new allies for a future multipolar world order as the partnership between the US and the European powers slides into a crisis.

entries.title: The sea that is vanishing in real time
entries.snippet: The world's largest inland body of water is retreating at an alarming speed. From stranded buildings to vanishing habitats, scientists warn the Caspian Sea may be approaching a tipping point.
